heading: Example 7.1
text:
Problem: An object travels 16 m in 4 s
and then another16 m in2 s. What is
the average speed of the object?
Formulas: average speed = total distance / total time
Solution:
Total distance travelled by the object =
16 m + 16 m = 32 m
Total time taken = 4 s + 2 s = 6 s
Average speed = Total distancetravelled / Total timetaken
= 32 m / 6 s = 5.33 m/s
Answer: 5.33m/s m/s

heading: Example 7.2
text:
Problem: The odometer of a car reads
2000 km at the start of a trip and
2400 km at the end of the trip. If the
trip took8 h, calculate the average
speed of the car in km/h and m/s.
Formulas: average speed = total distance / total time
Solution:
s = 2400 km - 2000 km = 400 km
Time elapsed, t = 8 h
v_av = s / t = 400 km / 8 h
= 50 km/h
= 50 * (1000 m / 1 km) * (1 h / 3600 s)
= 13.9 m/s
Answer: 50km/h km/h

heading: Example 7.3
text:
Problem: Usha swims in a 90 m long
pool. She covers180 m in one minute
by swimming from one end to the other
and back along the same straight path.
Find the average speed and average
velocity of Usha.
Formulas: average speed = total distance / total time
Solution:
Displacement of Usha in1 min = 0 m
Average speed = Total distancecovered / Totaltimetaken
= (180 m / 1 min) * (1 min / 60 s)
= 3 m/s
Average velocity = Displacement / Totaltimetaken
= 0 m / 60 s
= 0 m/s
Answer: 0m/s m/s

heading: Example 7.4
text:
Problem: Starting from a stationary
position, Rahul paddles his bicycle to
attain a velocity of6 m/s in30 s. Then
he applies brakes such that the velocity
of the bicycle comes down to4 m/s in
the next5 s. Calculate the acceleration
of the bicycle in both the cases.
Solution:
initial velocity, u = 0 ;
final velocity, v = 6 m/s ;
time, t = 30 s .
v-u (v-u) / a =
a = (6 m/s -0 m/s) / 30 s
= 0.2 m s-2
initial velocity, u = 6 m/s;
final velocity, v = 4 m/s;
time, t = 5 s.
Then, a = (4 m/s -6 m/s) / 5 s
= -0.4 m s-2 .
Answer: 4m/s m/s

heading: Example 7.5
text:
Problem: A train starting from rest
attains a velocity of72 km/h in5
minutes. Assuming that the acceleration
is uniform, find (i) the acceleration and
(ii) the distance travelled by the train for
attaining this velocity.
Solution:
u = 0 ; v = 72 km/h = 20 m/s and
t = 5 minutes = 300 s.
a= (v-u) / t
= (20 m/s - 0 ms-1) / 300 s
= (1/15) m s-2
2 as = v2 - u2 = v2 - 0
s = v2 / 2 a
= (20 ms-1)2 / 2*(1/15) ms-2
= 3000 m
= 3 km
Answer: 20m/s m/s

heading: Example 7.6
text:
Problem: A car accelerates uniformly
from18 km/h to36 km/h in5 s.
Calculate (i) the acceleration and (ii) the
distance covered by the car in that time.
Solution:
u = 18 km/h = 5 m/s
v = 36 km/h = 10 m/s and
t = 5 s .
v-u (v-u) / a=
= (10 m/s - 5 m/s) / 5 s
= 1 m s-2
s = u t + (1/2) a t2
= 5 m/s * 5 s + (1/2) * 1 m s-2 * (5 s)2
= 25 m + 12.5 m
= 37.5 m
Answer: 5m/s m/s

heading: Example 7.7
text:
Problem: The brakes applied to a car
produce an acceleration of6 m s-2 in the
opposite direction to the motion. If the
car takes2 s to stop after the application
of brakes, calculate the distance it
travels during this time.
Formulas: v = u + at
Solution:
a = - 6 m s-2 ; t = 2 s and v = 0 m/s.
v = u + at
0 = u + (- 6 m s-2) * 2 s
or u = 12 m/s .
s = u t + (1/2) a t2
= (12 m/s ) * (2 s) + (1/2) (-6 m s-2 ) (2 s)2
= 24 m - 12 m
= 12 m
Answer: 12m/s m/s
